title: Footprints Without Feet – Flashcard Concept Mastery
author: H.G. Wells

questions.question: Who is Griffin?
questions.correct_answer: A scientist who discovers how to make himself invisible
questions.explanation: Griffin is the central character whose scientific discovery of invisibility drives the entire story.

questions.question: How did Griffin become invisible?
questions.explanation: Through scientific experiments, Griffin makes his body transparent like glass.

questions.question: Why were footprints visible even though Griffin was invisible?
questions.correct_answer: Because his feet were covered with mud
questions.explanation: The mud made his footprints visible despite his invisible body.

questions.question: Why did Griffin remove his clothes while escaping?
questions.explanation: Only Griffin’s body was invisible, so he had to remove his clothes to escape unseen.

questions.question: Why did Griffin go to a big store in London?
questions.correct_answer: To find warmth, food, and clothes
questions.explanation: It was winter, and Griffin needed protection from the cold.

questions.question: Why did Griffin go to Iping village?
questions.correct_answer: To live in solitude and continue his work
questions.explanation: He wanted to escape crowded London and avoid suspicion.

questions.question: Why did Mrs Hall think Griffin was eccentric?
questions.correct_answer: Because of his strange appearance and behaviour
questions.explanation: Griffin avoided people and always covered his face, making him seem odd.

questions.question: What strange events occurred at the inn?
questions.correct_answer: Furniture moved on its own and money disappeared
questions.explanation: Griffin’s invisibility caused mysterious incidents that frightened people.

questions.question: Why did the villagers suspect Griffin of theft?
questions.correct_answer: He suddenly had money after claiming to be broke
questions.explanation: The timing of the theft and Griffin’s behaviour raised suspicion.

questions.question: What message does the story convey?
questions.correct_answer: Science without ethics can be dangerous
questions.explanation: Griffin’s misuse of scientific power leads to fear and destruction.

notes.title: Invisibility
notes.content: Invisibility gives Griffin power but also isolates him from society.

notes.title: Ethics in Science
notes.content: Scientific discoveries must be guided by moral responsibility.

facts.fact: Griffin’s invisibility is achieved through scientific experimentation.
Fear and suspicion drive the villagers’ reaction to Griffin.
